steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The final sentence signals the passage's structure: “they are hopeful that this discovery could aid in the development of new antibiotics for human use.” This isn't just describing the ants — it's indicating where the finding points, toward further research and application in human medicine.

steps.title: Match the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A correctly identifies the passage's two-part organization: the first part summarizes the research findings (the ants' ability to treat wounds and the 90% mortality reduction), and the second part identifies an area for further research (developing new human antibiotics based on this discovery).

The other choices misread the passage:
  • B falsely claims research methods are detailed
  • C falsely introduces evolutionary speculation
  • D frames the structure as the ants solving their own infection problem
, rather than the passage pointing toward human medical applications.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
